PyTorch torch.outer expects input first then vec2, while paddle.outer uses x and y; but documentation implies x corresponds to input and y to vec2. To align semantics, both must compute x.outer(y) = input.outer(vec2), so x should be [1], y should be [100] in Paddle, matching input=[1], vec2=[100] in PyTorch.
